AD_COLUMN.NAME: Creation Date
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: The date that this record is completed.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Created field indicates the date that this record was created.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Creation Date
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: The date that this record is completed.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Created field indicates the date that this record was created.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Updated By
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: User who updated this records
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Updated By field indicates the user who updated this record.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Client
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: Client for this installation.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: A Client is a company or a legal entity. You cannot share data between Clients.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Reference No.
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: The number for a specific reference.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The reference number can be printed on orders and invoices to allow your business partner to faster identify your records.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Organization
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: Organizational entity within client
AD_COLUMN.HELP: An organization is a unit of your client or legal entity - examples are store, department. You can share data between organizations.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Warehouse Rule Type
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: A distinct item characteristic used for processes and sometimes grouped within a category.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Type indicates the type of validation that will occur. This can be SQL, Java Script or Java Language.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Organization
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: Organizational entity within client
AD_COLUMN.HELP: An organization is a unit of your client or legal entity - examples are store, department. You can share data between organizations.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Created By
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: User who created this records
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Created By field indicates the user who created this record.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Active
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: A flag indicating whether this record is available for use or de-activated.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: There are two methods of making records unavailable in the system: One is to delete the record, the other is to de-activate the record. A de-activated record is not available for selection, but available for reporting. There are two reasons for de-activating and not deleting records: (1) The system requires the record for auditing purposes. (2) The record is referenced by other records. E.g., you cannot delete a Business Partner, if there are existing invoices for it. By de-activating the Business Partner you prevent it from being used in future transactions.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Updated By
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: User who updated this records
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Updated By field indicates the user who updated this record.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Payment Method
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: It is the method by which payment is expected to be made or received.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Created By
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: User who created this records
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Created By field indicates the user who created this record.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Record ID
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: An record identifier in the dictionary.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Record ID is the internal unique identifier of a record

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Updated
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: The date that this record was last updated
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Updated field indicates the date that this record was updated.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Result
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: The Result indicates the result of any action taken on this request.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Result indicates the result of any action taken on this request.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Active
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: A flag indicating whether this record is available for use or de-activated.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: There are two methods of making records unavailable in the system: One is to delete the record, the other is to de-activate the record. A de-activated record is not available for selection, but available for reporting. There are two reasons for de-activating and not deleting records: (1) The system requires the record for auditing purposes. (2) The record is referenced by other records. E.g., you cannot delete a Business Partner, if there are existing invoices for it. By de-activating the Business Partner you prevent it from being used in future transactions.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Client
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: Client for this installation.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: A Client is a company or a legal entity. You cannot share data between Clients.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Financial Account
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: Financial account used to deposit / withdrawal money such as bank accounts or petty cash
AD_COLUMN.HELP: Financial account used to deposit / withdrawal money such as bank accounts or petty cash

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Updated
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: The date that this record was last updated
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Updated field indicates the date that this record was updated.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Document Type
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: A value defining what sequence and process setup are used to handle this document.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Document Type determines document sequence and processing rules
